title: Gentiana neomandonii
intro: Gentiana neomandonii, also known as Chinese gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of gastritis. The bioactive constituents of Gentiana neomandonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and xanthones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Gentiana neomandonii is often prepared as an herbal tea or extract, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and formulas that promote digestive health and relieve gastrointestinal discomfort.
taxonomy: Gentiana neomandonii is classified within the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the gentian family. This family consists of approximately 1,700 species of flowering plants found mainly in tropical and temperate regions. Gentiana neomandonii, as a member of the Gentianaceae family, falls under the order Gentianales. Within the order Gentianales, Gentiana neomandonii belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, also known as the flowering plants. The classification of Gentiana neomandonii is further detailed by its kingdom Plantae, which encompasses all vascular plants.
